title: Add tie-breaking edge case test for cluster merge
analysis: The api_merge_clusters route (review.py:394-461) selects the survivor via `max(parsed_clusters, key=lambda c: c['clip_count'])`. Python's max() returns the first element with the maximum value when ties occur. Since parsed_clusters preserves the request's cluster_ids order (body.cluster_ids -> clusters_by_id lookup -> list comprehension at line 413-415), the first cluster ID in the request survives a tie. The existing test_merge_uses_batch_fetch seeds c-1 (1 clip) vs c-2 (2 clips), always selecting c-2 unambiguously. This task adds a test variant where both clusters have equal clip counts (2 each) to document and verify the tie-breaking behavior. No production code changes are needed -- this is a test-only task that pins the existing implicit contract.

steps.description: Write test_merge_equal_size_clusters_first_in_request_wins in TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch. Seed c-1 and c-2 each with 2 clips (c-1: [clip-a, clip-b], c-2: [clip-c, clip-d]). Request merge with cluster_ids=['c-1','c-2']. Assert: survivor is c-1 (first in request order), merged clip_ids contain all 4 clips sorted == ['clip-a','clip-b','clip-c','clip-d'], batch_args has 2 calls, batch_args[0]==['c-1','c-2'], batch_args[1]==['c-1'] (re-fetch of survivor). Reuse the same N+1 trap + batch spy monkeypatch pattern from test_merge_uses_batch_fetch.

Verify the test passes against existing production code (no production changes needed). Run pytest tests/test_cluster_review.py::TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch to confirm both the new tie-breaking test and the existing test are green. If the test fails, investigate whether max() tie-breaking differs from expectation and adjust the test assertion to match actual deterministic behavior.

design_decisions.rationale: Python's max() returns the first element with the maximum key value. Since parsed_clusters is built preserving body.cluster_ids order (review.py:413-415), the first cluster ID in the request is the deterministic survivor. This is a stable behavior worth pinning via test.

design_decisions.decision: Use distinct clip IDs (clip-a/b/c/d) instead of overlapping with existing test
design_decisions.rationale: Avoids confusion with the existing test's clip-1/2/3 IDs and makes it obvious which clips came from which cluster in the merged result.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse the same monkeypatch pattern (N+1 trap + batch spy) from the existing test
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the test class consistent and verifies the batch-fetch path is also used for the equal-size case. Both assertions (no N+1, correct batch calls) remain valuable.

design_decisions.decision: Test-only task, no production code changes
design_decisions.rationale: The tie-breaking behavior is already correct and deterministic via max() semantics. The task is to document this contract via test, not to change behavior.

reuse.how: Call with custom cluster_id and clip_ids_json to seed equal-size clusters

reuse.what: N+1 trap + batch spy monkeypatch pattern
reuse.how: Replicate the same _n1_trap / _batch_spy closures and monkeypatch.setattr calls into the new test method
Same pytest fixtures as the existing test method (already available via class method signature)
